---
title: "icosphere_cross_level_adjacency"
generated: true
description: "Coarse-to-fine adjacency ELL for icosphere pooling."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.sparse.mesh`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:icosphere_cross_level_adjacency -->
```python
icosphere_cross_level_adjacency(hierarchy: IcosphereHierarchy, coarse_level: int, fine_level: int) -> ELL
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:icosphere_cross_level_adjacency -->
Coarse-to-fine adjacency ELL for icosphere pooling.

Row `i` (coarse vertex at level `coarse_level`) carries the
fine vertices at level `fine_level` that descend from it under
the subdivision rule:

- The coarse-original fine vertex (same index `i`), and
- Every fine vertex that is a midpoint of an edge `(i, j)`
  incident to `i` in the coarse mesh.

The stored `values` are a `1.0 / 0.0` validity indicator: `1`
at real (vertex, child) entries and `0` at padding.  This serves
both downstream consumers without a per-consumer rebuild:

- [`mesh_pool_max`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/mesh_pool_max.qmd) ignores the values (it
  substitutes the `TROPICAL_MAX_PLUS` identity internally), so the
  indicator is harmless there.
- [`mesh_coarsen_meanpool`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/mesh_coarsen_meanpool.qmd) reads the indicator
  directly as the per-edge weight and as the per-row count, so a
  row-mean over the real children falls out for free.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:icosphere_cross_level_adjacency -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `hierarchy` | `IcosphereHierarchy` | — | — | An [`IcosphereHierarchy`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/IcosphereHierarchy.qmd) produced by [`icosphere_hierarchy`](../nitrix-sparse-mesh/icosphere_hierarchy.qmd). |
| `coarse_level` | `int` | — | — | Must be consecutive: `fine_level == coarse_level + 1`. Multi-level pooling is composed by the consumer. |
| `fine_level` | `int` | — | — | Must be consecutive: `fine_level == coarse_level + 1`. Multi-level pooling is composed by the consumer. |

**Returns** `ELL`. An [`ELL`](../nitrix-sparse-ell/ELL.qmd) of shape `(n_coarse, n_fine)` with `k_max = 1 + max_coarse_degree` (6 for the base icosahedron, 7 for every subdivided level). `values` carry a `1.0 / 0.0` validity indicator (real entries / padding); `identity` is the `REAL` identity `0.0`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
